react.answer: ## Building a Tooltip in React **Mental model:** A tooltip is a small overlay shown on hover *and* focus, after a short delay, anchored to a trigger. Wrap the trigger in a `position: relative` span and absolutely-position the bubble against it. The delay timer is the one piece of imperative state — keep it in a `ref` and always clear it. ### Step 1 — Trigger on hover and focus Keyboard users never hover, so handle `focus`/`blur` alongside `mouseenter`/`mouseleave`. ```jsx {children} ``` ### Step 2 — Debounce the appearance Show after `delay` ms; cancel the pending timer on hide so a quick pass-through shows nothing. ```jsx const timer = useRef(null); const show = () => { timer.current = setTimeout(() => setOpen(true), delay); }; const hide = () => { clearTimeout(timer.current); setOpen(false); }; ``` ### Step 3 — Position the bubble Absolutely position it above the trigger and center it with `translateX(-50%)`. ```jsx {text} ``` ### Step 4 — Clean up the timer A pending timeout firing after unmount would set state on a dead component. ```jsx useEffect(() => () => clearTimeout(timer.current), []); ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Quick mouse pass | `clearTimeout` on leave cancels before it shows. | | Keyboard focus | `onFocus`/`onBlur` cover non-mouse users. | | Viewport edges | A production version flips placement when it would overflow. | **Interview tip:** Bringing up focus/blur (not just hover) unprompted shows you think about keyboard accessibility — it's the most common thing candidates forget on a tooltip.

vue.answer: ## Building a Tooltip in Vue 3 **Mental model:** A tooltip shows a small overlay on hover *and* focus, after a short delay, anchored to a trigger. Wrap the trigger in a `position: relative` span and absolutely-position the bubble. Expose the text via a prop and the trigger via a ``. The delay timer is plain plumbing — clear it on hide and unmount. ### Step 1 — Slot the trigger, prop the text A slot keeps the component reusable with any trigger element. ```vue ``` ### Step 2 — Debounce the appearance Show after `delay` ms; cancel on hide. ```js let timer = null; function show() { timer = setTimeout(() => (open.value = true), props.delay); } function hide() { clearTimeout(timer); open.value = false; } ``` ### Step 3 — Position the bubble Center it above the trigger with `translateX(-50%)`. ```vue {{ text }} ``` ### Step 4 — Clean up on unmount ```js onUnmounted(() => clearTimeout(timer)); ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Quick mouse pass | `clearTimeout` cancels before it shows. | | Keyboard focus | `focusin`/`focusout` bubble from the slotted trigger. | | Viewport edges | Production code flips placement on overflow. | **Interview tip:** Use `focusin`/`focusout` (which bubble) rather than `focus`/`blur` (which don't) when the focusable element is *inside* the slot — that detail trips up a lot of Vue candidates.

angular.answer: ## Building a Tooltip in Angular **Mental model:** Show a small overlay on hover *and* focus, after a short delay, anchored to a projected trigger. Wrap the trigger (via ``) in a `position: relative` host span and absolutely-position the bubble. The delay timer is a plain field — clear it on hide and in `ngOnDestroy`. Classic `NgModule` setup, no `?.`/`??` (the build rejects them). ### Step 1 — Project the trigger, input the text ```typescript @Input() text = ''; @Input() delay = 200; open = false; private timer: any = null; ``` ### Step 2 — Trigger on hover and focus Use `mouseenter`/`mouseleave` and `focusin`/`focusout` (the latter bubble from the projected element). ```html ``` ### Step 3 — Debounce the appearance ```typescript show(): void { this.timer = setTimeout(() => { this.open = true; }, this.delay); } hide(): void { clearTimeout(this.timer); this.open = false; } ``` ### Step 4 — Position and clean up Center the bubble above the trigger, and clear the timer on destroy. ```typescript ngOnDestroy(): void { clearTimeout(this.timer); } ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Quick mouse pass | `clearTimeout` cancels before it shows. | | Keyboard focus | `focusin`/`focusout` cover non-mouse users. | | Destroy while pending | `ngOnDestroy` clears the timer. | **Interview tip:** Prefer `focusin`/`focusout` over `focus`/`blur` here — they bubble, so they fire even though the focusable element lives inside the projected content rather than on the host span.
